Current Date: Roughly mid-to-late 2024.
Current Bitcoin price (as of May/June 2024) is roughly $65,000 - $70,000.
The question is whether it reaches $150,000 in 2025.
This requires an increase of slightly more than 2x (from ~70k to 150k) in roughly 18 months, or within the next 18 months.
Historically, post-halving years (2013, 2017, 2021) have seen significant bull runs. The last halving was in April 2024. Post-halving peaks often occur 12-18 months later, which would be late 2024 or 2025.
In 2021 (post 2020 halving), BTC went from around $10k (mid 2020)/ $29k (early 2021) to $69k.
150k is often cited as a target for the cycle peak (e.g., Cathie Wood, Standard Chartered, Matrixport, PlanB).
However, diminishing returns suggest each cycle's multiplier from the previous all-time high is lower.
Previous ATH was ~$69k in 2021. Peak of the 2024-2025 cycle might reasonably be around $100k-$120k. $150,000 would be a > 2x from previous ATH.
Given options pricing and standard crypto volatility, a 2x increase on a 1.5-year horizon has a decent implied probability (often delta of maybe 20-30%).
For example, if current price is $70k, volatility ~60% annualized. Time to end of 2025 = 1.5 years.
A log-normal distribution would put the probability of *hitting* $150k (which is the maximum over the interval, not just European option at expiry) significantly higher than the European call probability.
Wait, reaching 150k *at any time* in 2025.
Let's approximate:
T = 1.5 years. Drift = maybe 10-20%? No, drift is often assumed risk-free rate in risk-neutral pricing. But for real-world probability, let's use a bit higher drift.
Using volatility of 50%, the probability of touching a barrier that is 2.14x the current price in 1.5 years:
For a geometric Brownian motion, P(max_{0 \to T} S_t >= B) = something we can approximate.
If S0 = 65k, B = 150k. Ratio = 2.3. ln(Ratio) = 0.83.
Vol = 0.6. Vol * sqrt(T) = 0.6 * 1.22 = 0.73.
Distance = 0.83 / 0.73 = 1.13 standard deviations.
With drift, touching probability is around 2 * P(Z > 1.13) = 2 * 13% = 26%.
Let's assign a probability of around 25%. Polymarket might have it around 20-30%. I'll forecast 0.25.
